# Cyclops

> Minimal Python agent framework. Any LLM. MCP native. Built on LiteLLM.

Cyclops is a thin Python wrapper around LiteLLM that provides agents, tool calling, streaming, structured output, memory, MCP, and OpenTelemetry observability. Works with every model LiteLLM supports.

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/getting-started/): Install, set API keys, run first agent, add tools
- [Agents](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/agents/): AgentConfig options, multi-turn history, reset, arun
- [Tools](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/tools/): @tool decorator, type annotations as schema, tool loop
- [Streaming](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/streaming/): stream() and astream() for token-by-token output
- [Structured Output](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/structured-output/): response_model with Pydantic models
- [Memory](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/memory/): InMemoryStorage and FileStorage for persistent key-value context
- [MCP](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/mcp/): MCPClient to connect servers, MCPServer to expose tools
- [Plugins](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/plugins/): cyclops-toolkit-* packages auto-discovered at runtime
- [Cost Tracking](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/cost-tracking/): run_with_response() returns tokens and estimated USD cost
- [Hooks](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/hooks/): AgentHooks subclass to observe or block LLM calls and tool executions
- [Observability](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/guides/observability/): TelemetryHooks for OpenTelemetry spans, works with Jaeger/Tempo/Datadog
- [API Reference](https://cyclops.gopalji.me/api-reference/): Agent, AgentConfig, AgentHooks, TelemetryHooks, storage classes, MCP classes
